Primitive Scientific.name: Product Safety Issue - B0BKH45VZM (US)
Primitive Scientific.text_content:
We received a compliance notice from Amazon informing the seller that a specific product listing (ASIN: B0BKH45VZM) has been removed due to a safety concern under its Dietary Supplements policy. The flagged product Primitive Scientific Nordic Pure Oil Complex is considered potentially non-compliant, likely due to issues such as ingredient safety, labeling inaccuracies, or lack of proper certification.

Amazon explains that the removal resulted from its internal review process, which identified possible risks to customer health. We have 10 business days to obtain details about the violation and how to resolve it. Required corrective action may include submitting documentation from accredited Testing, Inspection, and Certification (TIC) labs to prove product safety and compliance.
STATUS: Inactive
Available (FBA) - 139 units

Joymode.name: Investigate Coupon Ineligibility for Joymode Testosterone ASINs
Joymode.text_content:
Two Joymode Testosterone products that were previously eligible for Amazon Coupons are now showing the status "Unsafe to advertise", preventing coupon creation.
Amazon is displaying the following message:
"This ASIN is identified as potentially embarrassing or offensive and cannot be added to a coupon."
Affected ASINs:
 B0CP6BNZKM – JOYMODE Testosterone Support Complex (180ct) Pack of 2 
 B09XFFXPV2 – Testosterone Booster for Men (90 Capsules)
Please review these with Amazon for a fix

Nusava.name: Update B12 SEC Images | EBC
Nusava.text_content:
SEC:
EBC: 

Amazon's Prohibited/Misleading Claims

Amazon's PDP Requirements:

Include clear images of the product label with the Supplement Facts Panel.
Match all weights to the Supplement Facts Panel.
Remove any raw material weight claims.
Include ingredient names with their exact weights.
Specify portion sizes (per serving/tablet/bottle) with weights.

Added from Jasmin:
Please see the flags as per Amazon's guidelines on Misleading/Prohibited & Structure/Function claims and PDP requirements. Feel free to suggest alternative revisions.

EBC IMAGE 1 — "Simplify Your Health. Amplify Your Life."
   Flag: "Natural Energy • Mindful Balance • Daily Rhythm" — implied benefit claims without asterisks.
   Reason: These imply the product delivers these outcomes. Per Amazon policy, structure/function claims require the FDA disclaimer asterisk.
   Revision: Natural Energy* • Mindful Balance* • Daily Rhythm* — and ensure the FDA disclaimer appears somewhere in the EBC module.

EBC IMAGE 2 — "Clean, Conscious, and Convenient"
   Flag: "easy to use, effective formulas"
   Reason: "Effective" is an unqualified performance claim implying guaranteed results, which requires verifiable substantiation per Amazon's accuracy and verifiability requirement.
   Revision: "easy to use, thoughtfully formulated" or "easy to use, carefully crafted formulas"

EBC IMAGE 4 — "From Morning Rush to Evening Reset"
   Flag: "1 ½ dropper a day helps you stay on track to achieve your goals"
   Reason: Directly links the supplement to achieving personal goals, which is an implied performance outcome claim without qualification.
   Revision: "1 ½ dropper a day as part of your daily wellness routine*

EBC IMAGE 5 — Reviews / "Real Support for Real Routines"
Two flags:

   Flag 1: Customer review displayed reads "The vitamin content is helpful to my digestive system"
   Reason: Displaying a customer review that contains a health claim (digestive system benefit) in EBC content can be treated the same as a seller-made claim by Amazon. Digestive health claims require substantiation.
   Revision: Replace with a review that speaks to taste, convenience, or routine — not a body system claim.

   Flag 2: "Just one drop at a time" — serving size per SFP is 1.5 droppers (1.5 ml), not one drop.
   Reason: Inconsistent with the SFP serving size. This could be flagged as a misleading dosage representation.
   Revision: "Just 1½ droppers a day" or "One simple daily routine"
__________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
SEC IMAGE 1 — "Trusted by Thousands! Real routines. Real results."
Two Flags
   Flag 1: "Real results"
   Reason: Implies guaranteed outcomes for all users, which is an unqualified performance claim.
   Revision: "Real routines. Real support."

   Flag 2: "Trusted by Thousands"
   Reason: "Thousands" is a vague, unverifiable social proof claim. Amazon requires claims to be accurate and backed by verifiable evidence. If customer count cannot be confirmed at 2,000+, this is misleading. The earlier version "Over 3,000 Nusava Faithfuls" is more defensible as it gives a specific, substantiatable number.
   Revision: "Trusted by Over 3,000 Customers" — update the number as your customer base grows and keep sales/review data on hand to substantiate it.

SEC IMAGE 2 — "It Starts Simple. Then it Becomes Routine."
Three flags — highest risk image in the full set:

   Flag 1: "No more midday crashes"
   Reason: Phrased as a guaranteed elimination of a physical symptom, not a support claim. This is outcome language that requires substantiation.
   Revision: "Support for steady energy through the afternoon*"

   Flag 2: "Real results you can feel"
   Reason: Guaranteed performance claim. Combined with the specific outcome promises in each time block, this reads as a definitive product promise.
   Revision: "Simple habits, consistent support*"

   Flag 3: Typo — "Fell balanced, not drained"
   Reason: Copy error that looks unprofessional and could confuse customers.
   Revision: "Feel balanced, not drained*"

SEC IMAGE 5 — "Why B Vitamins?"
   Flag: "Liquid form for better absorption"
   Reason: "Better" is an unqualified comparative with no cited reference point or study. Same issue as the absorption chart from the main images.
   Revision: "Liquid form for convenient daily use" or "Liquid form for easy sublingual delivery"

SEC IMAGE 6 — "Low Energy During the Day?"
   Flag: Problem-solution framing — "Energy drops mid-day" paired with "Support steady energy throughout your day"
   Reason: Directly implies the product resolves the listed symptoms. The pairing of a symptom list with a product solution claim implies therapeutic benefit without an asterisk or qualifier.
   Revision: Add asterisk to "Support steady energy throughout your day*" and add the FDA disclaimer footer. Change "DAILY SUPPORT FOR ENERGY AND FOCUS" to "DAILY SUPPORT FOR ENERGY AND FOCUS*"

TT - Nusava.name: Account Health Weekly Metrics Reporting
TT - Nusava.text_content:
On the OB-Revised sheet, please make sure to fill in the numbers for the following metrics assigned to account support: 
Line 82 | Issues with OB Catalog Health - 
Line 95 | PrimeMD Tiktok Seller Violations
Line 96 | Nusava TikTok Seller Violations 
Line 97 | Medchoice TikTok Seller Violations 

For each of these metrics, please indicate how many issues were recorded on our accounts regardless if the status are still open or resolved. 

Please make sure the metrics are complete every Monday, 12 PM PST.
